stories: Good morning Sean, it's Wednesday, the sixth of May 2026
Here's a fact to kick off your Wednesday: on this day in 1954, Roger Bannister became the first person to run a mile in under four minutes вЂ” at a track in Oxford
Sports commentators had called it physically impossible
Looking at your day вЂ” morning run from seven to eight, then pre-read reviews at nine
Two review blocks at ten: the FY26 GBSO Full Year Performance Review, then the Potash GBS PMO CLT update
The SAP duplicate PO call is at midday, lunch at twelve-thirty, and the Potash ERP Stabilisation Forum at one-thirty
A private appointment at two, Tax Diagnostic at three, one-to-one with Reg at three-thirty, then pre-work for the GBSLT Quarterly at four, and Big Rock Time to close out at five
In Singapore, PM Lawrence Wong's May Day speech continues to generate attention
Good evening Sean, it's Tuesday evening, the fifth of May, 2026
A fact worth noting: Carl Linnaeus ГўВЂВ” the man who gave every living thing its two-part Latin name ГўВЂВ” was born on this date in 1707
Three hundred and nineteen years on, his binomial system still names every species on Earth
Nine brings a pre-read review block, then at ten, Big Rock time focused on your FY26 GBSO full-year performance review
Midday has the Daily Update Call on BHP SAP purchase order conversions, then L5 AIP work through to half-one
In Singapore today, parliament passed the Cross-Border Railways Bill, enabling co-location of Customs, Immigration and Quarantine facilities at the upcoming Johor Bahru to Singapore RTS rail stations
The four-kilometre link remains on track to open by end of 2026
New Zealand's Prime Minister Luxon was also in town to sign a trade pact
